candidates.title: Search enters 4th day for 2 young brothers who fell into Manitoba lake
candidates.description: The search continues for a fourth day to find two young brothers who went missing in Manitoba's Nutimik Lake on Saturday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 16-year-old charged with killing a cat in Winnipeg
candidates.description: A 16-year-old has been charged with killing or injuring animals after Winnipeg police allege they found the youth holding a knife with a dead cat nearby on Monday night.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Former Toronto police officer Derek McCormick pleads guilty in Project South investigation
candidates.description: One of the Toronto police officers charged in the sweeping police corruption and organized crime investigation dubbed Project South pleaded guilty to two charges on Tuesday morning.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Toronto mayor, local councillors look to ban surveillance pricing in city
candidates.description: Surveillance pricing, where businesses use consumer data to offer different prices based on what they think each individual customer will pay, threatens to gouge Torontonians who are already struggling to afford the cost of living, says Mayor Olivia Chow. She's introducing a motion Monday to see if the city can ban the practice.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: A 67-year-old died in one of B.C.'s oldest and largest open water swim events. Here's what we know
candidates.description: The B.C. Coroners Service is investigating after a 67-year-old man was found dead during an open water swim event on B.C.'s Okanagan Lake this past weekend.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada joins global program building 6th-generation stealth fighter jet
candidates.description: The Canadian government has signed on to become the first observer of the U.K., Italy and Japan's program to build the next generation of stealth fighter jets by 2035. The commander of the Royal Canadian Air Force told CBC News "it's feasible to imagine a world" where Canada becomes a full partner and buys these jets in the future.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Wheelchair basketball player Kady Dandeneau named Canadian flag-bearer for Commonwealth Games
candidates.description: Wheelchair basketball player Kady Dandeneau and lawn bowler Ryan Bester will lead Canada’s delegation at the opening ceremony for the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, Scotland on Thursday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Goldfish the North Atlantic right whale spotted outside N.L. office window
candidates.description: There are only about 380 North Atlantic right whales in the world and earlier this month, Kristinn Skulason was able to see one just out his office window in Bay de Verde, a town in Newfoundland’s Conception Bay.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Liberal critic says N.L. running out of time on MOU deal as Wakeham, Fréchette meet in P.E.I.
candidates.description: Newfoundland and Labrador Premier Tony Wakeham and Quebec Premier Christine Fréchette will meet in Prince Edward Island on Tuesday to discuss ongoing negotiations to reach a new agreement for the sale and development of Churchill Falls power.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Carney slammed over lack of Gordie Howe bridge opening deal details
candidates.description: Opposition MPs are expressing increasing frustration over the lack of details about how a deal between Canada and the U.S. to open a long-awaited major border crossing will work, with several calling on Ottawa to release a copy of the agreement.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 2 Ottawa police officers accused of gender-based misconduct have quietly left force
candidates.description: Two Ottawa police officers quietly left the service this month after one of them was accused of sexual misconduct and the other was criminally charged for alleged intimate partner violence, CBC News has learned.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: What’s the personal story behind how you will vote in the Alberta separation referendum? Share it on CBC News
candidates.description: We're looking for several people to publish their perspectives on CBC. Not the same political commentators, but real people with real stakes who are willing to share the why behind how they feel.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: A Sask. artist's phone fell 1.1 km from a plane. They found it in a canola field in 'pristine' condition
candidates.description: Heather Cline and David Stanchuk say they were blown away when they were able to find the phone. They were even more surprised to find it undamaged.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Friends, family mourn 22-year-old killed in wrong-way crash with Sask. Roughrider
candidates.description: Friends and family are mourning Bhishma Rajyaguru, who ​​d​ied​ Saturday evening ​after ​Saskatchewan Roughrider​ Jayden Dalke crashed into his vehicle.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Kashechewan First Nation members evacuated 6 months ago await water testing results to return home
candidates.description: Evacuees from Kashechewan First Nation who have been away from their homes for more than six months are awaiting water testing results to learn if it’s safe to return to the fly-in Cree community.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Parks Canada should axe agreement with NunatuKavut in Labrador national park, says report
candidates.description: A recent report on the Akami-Uapishku-KakKasuak-Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve says Parks Canada is operating in a “complex and highly charged context” in Labrador — and recommends the federal agency end its existing agreement with the NunatuKavut Community Council (NCC).
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Lebanese army enters southern town after Israeli withdrawal
candidates.description: Lebanese army troops began deploying in the southern Lebanese town of Zawtar al-Gharbiyeh on Tuesday after Israeli forces withdrew from the area, a senior Lebanese security official said, in line with a U.S.-brokered plan.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Why Trump is threatening new 50% tariffs on Canadian exports right now
candidates.description: It may appear that U.S. President Donald Trump's move to hit many Canadian products with a new tariff of 50 per cent came out of the blue, but there are clear signs of what's driving the tactic.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters vow to remain in New Delhi after police use tear gas, batons
candidates.description: Supporters of India's youth-led "cockroach" movement faced police batons and tear gas as they tried to march to parliament to demand the education minister's resignation.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Judge to hear evidence against singer D4vd in killing, dismemberment of teen
candidates.description: A judge is set to decide at a hearing that opens Tuesday whether there is enough evidence to put singer D4vd on trial in the killing and dismemberment of a 14-year-old girl who authorities said had threatened to expose their relationship. The hearing will be the first public presentation of evidence against him.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Man stabs 2 tourists near the Acropolis in Athens, Greece
candidates.description: A man stabbed and injured two tourists Tuesday morning near the ancient Acropolis in Athens, Greece's top tourist attraction, police said.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: How the outbreak of an intestinal parasite in the U.S. unfolded
candidates.description: More than 30 American states have reported illnesses related to Cyclospora, an intestinal parasite that contaminates food that has come into contact with human feces. Here's how the investigation into the outbreak of cyclosporiasis unfolded.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Tate brothers to fight extradition to U.K., where both face charges of rape
candidates.description: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate will fight extradition to the United Kingdom, where the brothers are accused of rape and sex trafficking, their lawyer said on Monday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Tanker attacked in Strait of Hormuz following a 10th night of U.S. strikes on Iran
candidates.description: Iran attacked a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz early Tuesday, forcing its crew to abandon the ship even as the United States conducted yet-another round of airstrikes targeting the Islamic Republic over control of the key waterway.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Despite the war, the U.S. and Iran are working together to deport asylum seekers, lawsuit says
candidates.description: Even as the U.S. waged war with Iran and encouraged its citizens to take to the streets, the Trump administration was working with the Iranian regime to fast-track the deportations of dissidents, according to a new lawsuit.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump's new 50% tariffs to hit even CUSMA-compliant goods
candidates.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is moving to impose tariffs of 50 per cent on a wide range of Canadian exports, a dramatic ramping up of his trade war against his northern neighbour.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Who is Nolan Wells, and why has the Black Mississippi teen's death received widespread attention?
candidates.description: The death of Nolan Wells, a Black teenager whose body was located on an island off the Mississippi coast after he spent the Fourth of July there with three white friends, has drawn widespread attention amid mounting unanswered questions.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Fact check: Does this video show Iranian missiles hitting a U.S. base?
candidates.description: After Iranian missiles hit Jordan on Friday, this video circulated claiming to show missiles hitting a U.S. base in the country. But is the video real or AI generated?
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Women's Tennis Association introduces gene testing for all players on tour
candidates.description: The Women's Tennis Association is introducing one-off mandatory gene-testing for all players effective from Tuesday, a requirement to compete at the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 27 bodies recovered, dozens feared dead after passenger ferry sinks off coast of Guyana
candidates.description: Guyana's government said on Monday that 27 ‌bodies had been recovered from a ferry that capsized off the coast over the weekend with 179 people on board.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Messi says Argentina's World Cup final defeat to Spain left 'immense pain'
candidates.description: Lionel Messi says Argentina's World Cup final defeat by Spain had left "immense" pain but praised his team for reaching a second successive final and thanked supporters for uniting behind the national side.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Paramount, Warner must pause merger for at least two weeks, judge says
candidates.description: A federal judge on Monday ordered Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery to halt their $81 billion US merger for at least two weeks, allowing states that are challenging the deal more time to see their case through in court.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Meet Jimothy, the unusually round Seattle raccoon capturing hearts worldwide
candidates.description: A Seattle raccoon with an unusually short body nicknamed Jimothy has become an internet sensation after a video of the critter scampering across a field went viral.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ukraine fired 400 drones toward Moscow, city's mayor says
candidates.description: Ukraine fired more than 400 drones toward Moscow in its latest major attack on the Russian capital, the city's mayor said Monday. The same day, Ukrainian officials said a Russian missile strike on a ‌ship carrying corn near Ukraine's southern port of Odesa killed 10 people in the deadliest attack in a weeks-long flare-up of violence in the Black Sea.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Andy Burnham officially becomes British prime minister
candidates.description: Andy Burnham became the fifth British prime minister in four years and the seventh in a decade as he formally accepted an invitation from King Charles to form a government.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: The big stories — and best photos — from the 2026 World Cup
candidates.description: Forty-eight teams, 104 matches, 39 days. Here's a look back at some of the main storylines from the FIFA World Cup 2026 — as illustrated by some of the tournament's best photos.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada's inflation rate eased to 2.8% in June thanks to lower gas prices
candidates.description: While higher oil prices have sent the cost of gas up in recent months, the recent ceasefire in the Middle East led to a 10.2 per cent drop in the cost of gas month-over-month. Statistics Canada says inflation was unchanged from May to June when gas prices are taken out of the equation.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Unifor members ratify new deal with Ford Motor that includes wage increases, new investment
candidates.description: The union representing more than 5,000 workers at the Ford Motor Company of Canada has ratified a new three-year collective agreement — including a wage increase of three per cent per year; renewal of a cost-of-living allowance (COLA) and $1.2 billion in planned investments in Canadian manufacturing, according to details released by both the union and the company.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
candidates.description: Iran says it has hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Lebanese army says troops deploying in 'pilot zone' after Israeli withdrawal
candidates.description: A US-brokered agreement should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill 12, including family of six, medics say
candidates.description: Firas al-Masri, his wife and their four children died in one attack, which the Israeli military says targeted a Hamas operative.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Two French firefighters die tackling blaze near Bordeaux airport
candidates.description: The blaze close to the parking lots at Bordeaux-Mérignac Airport is said to have originated from a brush fire.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Brazil's Amazon sees sharp decline in size of area burned in 2025
candidates.description: The amount of land burned in the world's largest rainforest was the smallest since MapBiomas records began in 1985.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: India's 'cockroach' protest continues a day after police crackdown
candidates.description: Delhi witnessed overwhelming violence on Monday, with at least 60 protesters wounded. Police said 118 of their men were also injured.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Police officers arrested for allegedly seeking roadside bribe from Nigeria's anti-corruption boss
candidates.description: They are accused of stopping Musa Aliyu at a fake roadblock and telling him to drive to a cash machine.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: At least 27 dead and 83 missing after passenger ferry capsizes off Guyana
candidates.description: An investigation into the tragedy has been launched, the island's prime minister says, adding he hopes more will be found alive.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Tate brothers appear in US court for extradition proceedings
candidates.description: They were arrested on Saturday after the UK announced dozens of new charges, including rape and sex trafficking, which they deny.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia
candidates.description: The Houthis say the embargo begins immediately and is a response to a Saudi blockade of ports and airports in Yemen.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Notorious drug cartel leader Ismael Zambada sentenced to life in US prison
candidates.description: Known as "El Mayo", the co-founder the Sinaloa Cartel was also ordered to pay $15bn after he admitted to sending vast amounts of drugs to the US.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Why are thousands protesting for education reform in India?
candidates.description: Thousands of mostly young people tried to march on India's parliament to demand changes to how the education system is run.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Shakira, Madonna, Justin Bieber and BTS perform at colourful World Cup half-time show
candidates.description: Robbie Williams and Jennifer Hudson also performed, with Kylie Jenner and Timothee Chalamet among the celebrities in the crowds.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: ‘All I think about is her’: Families demand answers as Nigeria's boat disasters keep happening
candidates.description: Hundreds of lives are lost each year in Nigeria as a result of boat accidents yet nothing seems to change.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch Texas police officer pull man from burning car
candidates.description: Bodycam and dashcam footage shows a police officer rescue a man seconds before his car was engulfed in flames in Texas.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: From hundreds to thousands: How India's 'cockroach' protest movement has grown
candidates.description: BBC's Azadeh Moshiri reports as thousands attempt to march to India's parliament in support of the Cockroach Janta Party's demand for education reforms.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Why are Ukraine's military recruiters facing violence in their own country?
candidates.description: The latest National Police Service report recorded more than 600 assaults on recruitment officers to date and warned that the aggression has become “increasingly threatening”.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Thousands of websites taken down for illegal World Cup streams
candidates.description: More than 1,000 illegal streaming sites were shut down in the US during the tournament, with a similar amount blocked in Colombia.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: AliExpress fined record €550m by EU for allowing sale of illegal goods
candidates.description: The Chinese retailer allowed unsafe toys and counterfeit clothes to be sold on its site, says the EU.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Hosepipe ban in force for millions of households - is your area affected?
candidates.description: More than eight million households have been placed under hosepipe bans - which has raised questions about how effectively water resources are being managed.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Canadian wildfires rage as Trump warns Carney again over 'poisoning' US air
candidates.description: The US president raised the issue with the the Canadian prime minister while the pair attended the World Cup Final in New Jersey.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
candidates.description: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Iran-backed Houthis announce blockade. And, Cyclospora source remains unknown
candidates.description: Iran-backed Houthis say they will blockade a waterway on the Red Sea. And, the FDA continues to investigate Taylor Farms lettuce as a source of the Cyclospora outbreak despite a retracted test.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Amid U.S.-Iran war, Houthi rebels threaten to blockade another key strait
candidates.description: The Houthis, a Shia militia in Yemen that's aligned with Iran, have announced a naval blockade of a key waterway, the Bab el-Mandeb Strait.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Spain's World Cup champions return home for parade in front of almost 2 million
candidates.description: The players and their coaching staff met with the country's royals, and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez. An estimated crowd of almost 2 million was on hand to greet the squad through the streets of Madrid.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Powerball ticket sales begin Tuesday in the United Kingdom
candidates.description: Powerball ticket sales in the United Kingdom will begin Tuesday, giving players across the Atlantic a chance at winning the same massive jackpot that U.S. players already play for.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: The war in Iran has left hundreds of U.S. diplomats in limbo
candidates.description: As the U.S-Iran war ramps up again, hundreds of diplomats are in limbo. The State Department drew down staffing due to Iran's attacks on Gulf states. It's unclear if they'll return to normal business.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: The war with Iran is escalating. What next?
candidates.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Suzanne Maloney of the Brookings Institution about the U.S. war with Iran, the increase in hostilities and what it means for the wider region
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: India's Gen Z 'Cockroach People's Party' started as satire but is serious about change
candidates.description: They call themselves the "cockroaches" and they're on the march — demanding action against corruption, amid a shortage of opportunities for young people in India.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Putin meets North Korean foreign minister in diplomatic push
candidates.description: The visit by North Korean Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui comes without an obvious occasion, such as a multilateral diplomatic event or state anniversary, and has raised speculation that she may be coordinating a possible visit to Russia by North Korean leader Kim Jong Un.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: 3 U.S. personnel killed in Iran conflict. And, Hunter Biden opens up to NPR
candidates.description: Three U.S. service members have been killed since Friday amid rising tension between the U.S. and Iran. And, Hunter Biden talks to NPR's Newsmakers about addiction and public scrutiny.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian goods
candidates.description: President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Amid nurse shortage, a university rolls out the welcome mat for men
candidates.description: Only about 1 in 8 nurses are men, but their share is growing. A strong job market for nurses and sustained efforts to recruit men into the field have made a difference, including in the South.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Court ruling freezes Paramount-Warner Bros. merger for now
candidates.description: Paramount's owners were hoping they'd be firmly in control of rival Warner Bros. Discovery by the end of this week. Monday's temporary restraining order puts that plan on hold.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: FDA still focused on Taylor Farms lettuce as Cyclospora source despite retracted test
candidates.description: The FDA retracted its only positive test for cyclospora, the parasite at the center of a multistate outbreak, on Taylor Farms lettuce. But the agency believes lettuce from the brand is still linked.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Trump cuts to advisory groups leaves hospitals without key expertise on disease
candidates.description: Under the Trump administration, dozens of advisory committees have been ended or frozen, including one on controlling infection in healthcare settings.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: FDA still focused on Taylor Farms lettuce as Cyclospora source despite retracted test
candidates.description: The FDA says lettuce distributed by Taylor Fresh Foods and Taylor Farms de Mexico is still linked to the multistate outbreak of cyclosporiasis. But the source may be difficult to definitively trace.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: FDA retracts positive cyclospora test on Taylor Farms lettuce
candidates.description: The FDA says the test identifying cyclospora in Taylor Farms iceberg lettuce was a "false positive." But the lettuce is still linked epidemiologically to cases of the illness in a multistate outbreak.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: At least 930 people have died in Congo's Ebola outbreak
candidates.description: Congo's health ministry said the deaths were recorded out of the 2,344 cases confirmed as of Saturday. Thirty-seven new deaths were recorded in the previous 24 hours in one of the highest totals.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Deloitte-run systems denied Medicaid to disabled people. New laws could make it worse
candidates.description: Complex computer systems that decide whether people get Medicaid benefits have a history of errors. Now, changes to comply with Trump's tax and domestic policy law are inundating those systems.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: So distressed you can't think straight? Lower the intensity with these 4 skills
candidates.description: A clinical psychologist shares effective strategies to quickly de-escalate stress and anxiety. That includes dunking your head into a bowl of ice water to trigger your mammalian dive reflex.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: New study finds avocado oil products may contain cheaper oils
candidates.description: Sales of avocado oil and products made with it are booming as consumers willingly pay a premium, drawn by the oil's health halo. New research finds many products actually contain other, cheaper oils.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 21 at 1:48PM EDT by NWS Newport/Morehead City NC
candidates.description: At 148 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm near Harrells, or 13 miles west of Wallace, moving northeast at 40 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 50 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Wallace, Warsaw, Rose Hill, Beulaville, Kenansville, Potters Hill, Harrells, Sarecta, Magnolia, Greenevers, and Teachey.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Heat Advisory issued July 21 at 10:47AM PDT until July 22 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Portland OR
candidates.description: * WHAT...High temperature today of 95 to 98 degrees F. * WHERE...South Washington Cascade Foothills. * WHEN...Until 11 PM PDT Wednesday. * IMPACTS...Hot temperatures may cause heat illnesses. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Smoke from fires outside of the region or clouds moving into the area could result in cooler daytime high temperatures than currently forecast. Around a 10-15% chance of thunderstorms on Wednesday afternoon which may increase cloud cover and thus cause temperatures to be slightly lower than forecast.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Heat Advisory issued July 21 at 10:47AM PDT until July 21 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Portland OR
candidates.description: * WHAT...High temperature today of 95 to 98 degrees F. * WHERE...Cascade Foothills of Marion and Linn Counties and Linn County Lowlands. * WHEN...Until 11 PM PDT this evening. * IMPACTS...Hot temperatures may cause heat illnesses. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Smoke from area fires and incoming cloud cover may reduce temperatures in some locations. However, there remains a high probability of temperatures meeting Major HeatRisk today. This is especially true within the valleys.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Heat Advisory issued July 21 at 10:47AM PDT until July 22 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Portland OR
candidates.description: * WHAT...High temperatures up to 100. * WHERE...Greater Portland/Vancouver Metro, Western and Central Columbia River Gorge and the Hood River Valley, Lower Columbia River and Cowlitz River Valleys, and Central Willamette Valley. * WHEN...Until 11 PM PDT Wednesday. * IMPACTS...Hot temperatures may cause heat illnesses. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Smoke from fires outside of the region or clouds moving into the area could result in cooler daytime high temperatures than currently forecast. Around a 10-15% chance of thunderstorms on Wednesday afternoon which may increase cloud cover and thus cause temperatures to be slightly lower than forecast.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Flash Flood Warning issued July 21 at 10:47AM MST until July 21 at 1:45PM MST by NWS Tucson AZ
candidates.description: FFWTWC The National Weather Service in Tucson has issued a * Flash Flood Warning for... Southwestern Cochise County in southeastern Arizona... * Until 145 PM MST. * At 1047 AM MST, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing heavy rain across the warned area. Up to 1 inch of rain has fallen. Additional rainfall amounts up to 1 inch are possible in the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly. HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses as well as other poor drainage and low-lying areas. * Some locations that will experience flash flooding include... Sierra Vista and Charleston. This includes the following highways... Route 90 between mile markers 320 and 329. Route 92 between mile markers 322 and 326.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 21 at 1:46PM EDT by NWS Newport/Morehead City NC
candidates.description: At 145 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm over Hubert, or near Piney Green, moving northeast at 35 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Jacksonville, Piney Green, Stella, Swansboro, Hubert, Silverdale, Kuhns, and Peletier.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 21 at 1:46PM EDT until July 21 at 2:15PM EDT by NWS Baltimore MD/Washington DC
candidates.description: At 145 PM EDT, a severe thunderstorm was located near Schuyler, or 15 miles southeast of Waynesboro, moving east at 30 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and quarter size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Damaging winds will cause some trees and large branches to fall. This could injure those outdoors, as well as damage homes and vehicles. Roadways may become blocked by downed trees. Localized power outages are possible. Unsecured light objects may become projectiles. Locations impacted include... Schuyler, Wintergreen, Nellysford, Esmont, Lovingston, Bryant, Damon, North Garden, Covesville, Tyro, Rockfish, Greenfield, Alberene, Jonesboro, Faber, and Massies Mill.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 21 at 1:46PM EDT by NWS Wilmington OH
candidates.description: At 146 PM EDT, a strong thunderstorm was located near Marysville, moving east at 40 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 45 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Columbus, Dublin, Westerville, Delaware, Marysville, Powell, Johnstown, Sunbury, Utica, Polaris, New California, New Albany, Shawnee Hills, Galena, Ostrander, Alexandria, Hartford, Magnetic Springs, Alum Creek State Park, and Raymond. This includes I-71 in Ohio between mile markers 120 and 138. If outdoors, consider seeking shelter inside a sturdy building. A Severe Thunderstorm Watch remains in effect until 800 PM EDT for central and west central Ohio. To report hazardous weather conditions, go to our website at weather.gov/iln and submit your report via social media, when you can do so safely.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Flood Watch issued July 21 at 1:45PM EDT until July 22 at 8:00PM EDT by NWS Morristown TN
candidates.description: * WHAT...Flash flooding caused by excessive rainfall is possible. * WHERE...Portions of east Tennessee, including the following areas, Anderson, Blount Smoky Mountains, Campbell, Claiborne, Cocke Smoky Mountains, Grainger, Hamblen, Hancock, Hawkins, Jefferson, Johnson, Knox, Loudon, Morgan, North Sevier, Northwest Blount, Northwest Carter, Northwest Cocke, Northwest Greene, Roane, Scott TN, Sevier Smoky Mountains, Southeast Carter, Southeast Greene, Sullivan, Unicoi, Union and Washington TN and southwest Virginia, including the following areas, Lee, Russell, Scott VA, Washington VA and Wise. * WHEN...From midnight EDT tonight through Wednesday evening. * IMPACTS...Excessive runoff may result in flooding of rivers, creeks, streams, and other low-lying and flood-prone locations. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS... - Periods of showers and thunderstorms are expected tonight into Wednesday. Locations that receive multiple rounds or extended periods of locally heavy rainfall could experience flash flooding. Locations that have recently seen flooding need less rainfall to have additional flooding, and should take additional rounds of heavy rain and storms seriously. - http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 21 at 1:44PM EDT by NWS Tallahassee FL
candidates.description: At 144 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm near Crawfordville, moving north at 40 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Tallahassee, Tallahassee State College, Florida State University, Crawfordville, Florida A and M, Doak Campbell Stadium, Sopchoppy, Lake Bradford, Shadeville, Leon Sinks, Gaither Community Center, Lake Munson, Wakulla Springs, Walker Ford Community Center, South City, Eight Mile Pond, Medart, Buckhorn, Callen Neighborhood, and Bethel.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Patch Policy: Efficient Embodied Control via Dense Visual Representations
candidates.description: Pretrained dense visual features from Vision Transformers (ViTs) are powerful yet have been underutilized in robot learning. Modern robot policies either compress each observation into a single global token, or rely on visual backbones trained from scratch, sacrificing both fine-grained spatial detail and the benefits of large-scale visual pre-training. While there exist policies that do operate on dense patch features like large vision-language-action models (VLAs), they tend to be heavy and slow, inheriting the full cost of a billion-parameter vision-language model (VLM) backbone. We close this gap with Patch Policy, a minimal architectural extension that enables transformer-based policies to consume dense pre-trained patch tokens directly without the computational overhead of a full VLM. At its core is a block-causal attention mask that preserves the temporal causality of standard policies while letting the model attend over many patch tokens per observation, alongside other state information. Patch Policy is lightweight, fast, and highly effective. Across four simulated and three real-world environment suites, our method achieves a 40% relative improvement over policies using state-of-the-art global-pooled representations. Furthermore, it surpasses fine-tuned OpenVLA-OFT by 18% while using roughly 0.7% of the parameters. We believe Patch Policy provides a pipeline for the robotics community to readily leverage continuing progress in visual representation learning, without sacrificing the training efficiency or inference speed required for high-frequency, reactive control. Videos can be viewed at https://patch-policy.github.io
candidates.author: Gaoyue Zhou, Zichen Jeff Cui, Ada Langford, Bowen Tan, Yann LeCun, Lerrel Pinto

candidates.title: Automated Discovery Has No Universally Superior Harness
candidates.description: Autonomous discovery systems such as OpenEvolve and TTT-Discover are often used as general-purpose harnesses. However, in practice these are composite systems combining several design choices about archives, parent selection, exploration, and budget allocation into a single recipe. Because discovery runs are expensive and inherently stochastic, existing harnesses are often compared using too few independent trials to distinguish key methodological improvements from run-to-run variance. We systematically decompose OpenEvolve-style evolutionary search and the TTT-Discover search harness into its constituent components and systematically evaluate 30 budget-matched harnesses across 12 model-problem pairs using more than 3.1 million LLM rollouts and repeated-trial statistical analysis. Our results show that discovery harnesses have a generalization problem: No fixed harness is reliably superior across the evaluated model-problem pairs, and variants of OpenEvolve generally underperform simpler alternatives. Thus, harness choice is better viewed as a hyperparameter rather than as a universal recipe, and should be tailored to the specific problem and underlying model. We also find that early discovery progress predicts final performance, and use this property to present a budget-matched adaptive-allocation experiment that starts multiple harnesses, prunes weak partial runs, and reallocates compute to stronger survivors, outperforming both commitment to a randomly sampled fixed harness and a non-adaptive harness ensemble. Together, these results motivate shifting from fixed harness selection to online adaptation guided by early performance. We release all run pools including baseline null distributions for every model-problem pair as reusable statistical infrastructure against for future harness proposals.
candidates.author: Akshat Gupta, Jermaine Lei, Alexander Lu, Gopala Anumanchipalli, Leshem Choshen

candidates.title: It's Not What You Say, It's How You Say It: Evaluating LLM Responses to Expressions of Belief
candidates.description: Users frequently express their beliefs to large language models (LLMs). In some situations, the LLM should accept these contextual beliefs as true. In others, they should stick to their prior knowledge. Notably, users' expressions of belief (EoBs) can take linguistically diverse forms - using presuppositions, evidential and certainty markers, or varied tones - each of which may have a different persuasiveness over the LLMs. We introduce a typology to systematically evaluate how different EoBs affect whether models follow context versus prior knowledge. The typology is grounded in four linguistically motivated dimensions: form, evidentiality, epistemic stance, and tone, spanning 17 fine-grained types. By pairing these EoBs with world knowledge facts, we generate controlled EoB-query pairs that isolate the effect of linguistic variation. Using this benchmark, we evaluate 16 LLMs that differ in architecture (Llama3, Qwen3, Gemma3), scale (1B-30B parameters), and training stages (base vs instruct). We identify meaningful variations in response behavior across these axes, e.g., that bigger models and instruction models tend to be less context-following than smaller models and base models. We further identify specific EoBs that statistically significantly persuade LMs more consistently than others. Our work reveals systematic patterns in how linguistic framing affects LLM context integration, with implications for prompt engineering and model robustness.
candidates.author: Kevin Du, Clara Kümpel, Michelle Wastl, Alex Warstadt

candidates.title: FM-VLA: Force-based Memory for Vision-Language-Action Models in Contact-Rich Manipulation
candidates.description: Vision-language-action (VLA) models have achieved impressive generalization in robotic manipulation, and recent memory-augmented VLAs have relaxed the Markovian assumption by conditioning on past images or language summaries. Vision-based memory approaches address this by conditioning on sampled past image frames, but they are computationally expensive and fundamentally limited when temporal events are visually ambiguous, e.g., pushing a button multiple times with small movements. We propose FM-VLA, a VLA model with force-based memory, enabling temporal context reasoning for non-Markovian, contact-rich manipulation. We encode force histories into compact force memory tokens with a variational autoencoder (VAE) pretrained with force time series reconstruction. By projecting force latent representations and short state history as additional conditioning tokens to the action expert module, we enable VLAs to leverage accumulated contact event history to guide manipulation. We evaluate FM-VLA on three memory-dependent tasks, including finding a hidden block, pressing a button, and wiping a dish for a specific number of times. Our lightweight force memory achieves over 80% success rate with minimal inference overhead, significantly outperforming baseline approaches. Project page: https://qft-333.github.io/FM-VLA-Page/
candidates.author: Ruicheng Li, Qixiu Li, Ruichun Ma, Yu Deng, Lin Luo, Zhiying Du, Jianfeng Xiang, Huizhi Liang, Ruicheng Wang, Jiaolong Yang, Baining Guo

candidates.title: Simple Domain Generalization for Strong Pixel-Level Image Tampering Detection in Modern VLMs
candidates.description: Modern vision-language models (VLMs) have significantly improved image generation and editing capabilities, making pixel-level image tampering detection increasingly important yet challenging under cross-model and out-of-distribution shifts. This work studies domain generalization for pixel-level image tampering detection in modern VLMs like ChatGPT, Gemini, Qwen-Image, etc., aiming to learn tampering localization models that remain robust across diverse VLM-generated manipulation distributions. We propose a simple yet effective domain-generalized training framework built on two practical strategies. First, we introduce a balanced minibatch sampling scheme that strategically samples tampered and real images in each minibatch, preventing biased optimization toward either manipulated artifacts or clean-image priors and avoiding training collapse, ensuring that each optimization step receives proper sampled gradient signals. Second, we adopt a simple late-injection strategy, where the detector is first trained on large-scale base data until stable convergence, and then exposed to a small amount of newly selected supporting data from emerging VLM distributions, improving adaptability without overfitting to limited new domains. Together, these components provide a simple yet strong recipe for improving pixel-level tampering localization and OOD robustness across modern VLMs. Despite the conceptual simplicity, our framework outperforms the prior state-of-the-art PIXAR by a large margin of 26.1% and 26.8% relative improvement in average gIoU and cIoU, respectively, across OOD VLMs of GPT-Images-2.0, Gemini-3.1, FLUX.2, and Seedream 4.5. Our code is available at https://github.com/VILA-Lab/PIXAR-DG
candidates.author: Yi Tang, Xinyi Shang, Jiacheng Cui, Sondos Mahmoud Bsharat, Jiacheng Liu, Xiaohan Zhao, Tran Dinh Tien, Ahmed Elhagry, Salwa K. Al Khatib, Tianjun Yao, Yonina C. Eldar, Jing-Hao Xue, Hao Li, Salman Khan, Zhiqiang Shen

candidates.title: Logical Judgments Under Pressure: Diagnosing Syllogistic Stability with Learned Soft Prefixes
candidates.description: To test how correct logical judgments respond to learned context, we prepend a soft prefix to an exactly labeled syllogistic reasoning benchmark while keeping the model fixed. Soft prefixes are opaque continuous vectors, so we characterize them through the behavior they induce across controlled variations in logical form and interface. By studying which prefixes succeed and how their effects generalize, we characterize how learned contextual pressure can override correct judgments and expose limits in a model's logical stability. Across Qwen3.6-35B-A3B MoE, Qwen3-8B, and Gemma 4 31B, learned prefixes redirect many correct answers and remain effective across unseen forms and interface changes. In repeated tests with Qwen3.6 MoE and Gemma, they outperform paired random controls in all 16 model--direction--split comparisons by 37 to 99 percentage points. Qwen3.6 MoE flip rates remain between 72% and 90% across wording and prompt changes, while Gemma validity prefixes retain 54% to 56% flip compared with less than 1% for matched random prefixes. Diagnostic tests show that the dominant effect is a broad preference for one answer meaning rather than fixed-symbol forcing or a logical operation that transfers reliably between tasks. The form of this bias differs across models. In both Qwen models, simple score models often predict which judgments will flip but not how far their margins will move, whereas Gemma's overall response is more closely approximated by the same models. These results show that the dominant behavioral effect of successful soft prefixes is a broad answer preference, while the remaining response reveals substantial model-specific differences in logical stability.
candidates.author: Brian K Chen

candidates.title: GigaPath-Flash and GigaTIME-Flash: Efficient Pathology Foundation Models for Whole-Slide and Tumor Microenvironment Analysis
candidates.description: Foundation models have emerged as a driving force in computational pathology, with the potential to transform cancer diagnosis, prognosis, and treatment selection by learning transferable representations from large-scale histopathology data. A growing landscape of pathology foundation models now spans diverse data sources, architectures, and downstream applications. However, most pretrained models operate only at the image-tile level, use restrictive licenses, and remain computationally expensive, limiting large-scale slide-level clinical and research use. Here, we introduce GigaPath-Flash and GigaTIME-Flash, efficient models for whole-slide pathology AI and spatial proteomics prediction. GigaPath-Flash combines a 22M-parameter ViT-S tile encoder with a 21M-parameter LongNet slide encoder, both pretrained on large-scale real-world histopathology data. Its compact tile encoder is distilled from the billion-parameter GigaPath (ViT-g) teacher and shared by both models. GigaPath-Flash retains 97% of GigaPath's average slide-level performance with 50x less compute. GigaTIME-Flash extends this backbone to predict the tumor immune microenvironment directly from routine H&E images. It surpasses the original CNN-based GigaTIME in prediction quality while running 6x faster and using 8x less GPU memory. Together with GigaPath and GigaTIME, these models form an open-weight, Apache-2.0-licensed family pretrained on large-scale real-world clinical data. By releasing all models and weights, we provide accessible building blocks for computational pathology, immuno-oncology, and precision health.
candidates.author: Naoto Usuyama, Jeya Maria Jose Valanarasu, Sicong Yao, Hanwen Xu, Jaspreet Bagga, Guanghui Qin, Robert E. Kramer, Cliff Wong, Soohee Lee, Hao Qiu, Theodore Zhengde Zhao, Racheli Ben Shimol, Angela Crabtree, Kevin Matlock, Eduardo Alejandro Lozano Garcia, Naiteek Sangani, Alberto Santamaria-Pang, Jason Entenmann, Alexandra Q. Bartlett, Bill J. Wright, Bernard A. Fox, Brian Piening, Sheng Zhang, Sheng Wang, Tristan Naumann, Carlo Bifulco, Hoifung Poon

candidates.title: SWE-Pruner Pro: The Coder LLM Already Knows What to Prune
candidates.description: Pruning long context for coding agents has been a vital technology for efficient context management. While existing context pruning methods such as SWE-Pruner realize this by attaching a separate code classifier, we find the agent itself encodes internal representations indicating the relevance of code context when reading tool output. Based on this finding, we propose SWE-Pruner Pro, which prunes tool outputs directly inside the agent. Concretely, a small head turns the agent's own internal representations into a keep-or-prune label for each line, with a length-aware embedding keyed to each tool output's line count. Across two open-weight backbones and four multi-turn benchmarks, SWE-Pruner Pro saves up to 39% of prompt and completion tokens while preserving task quality, with bounded inference overhead. Notably, on MiMo-V2-Flash SWE-Pruner Pro additionally raises the SWE-Bench Verified resolve rate by +3.8% and the long-context Oolong accuracy by +2.2 points.
candidates.author: Yuhang Wang, Yuling Shi, Shaoqiu Zhang, Jialiang Liang, Shilin He, Siyu Ye, Yuting Chen, Kai Cai, Xiaodong Gu

candidates.title: Optimization of sim-to-real transfer in the humanoid robot NICO
candidates.description: Robotic grasping requires accurate coordination between visual perception, object localization, inverse kinematics, and hand control. However, when movements planned in simulation are executed on a physical robot, the sim-to-real gap can cause small positioning errors that prevent successful grasping. In our previous work, we introduced a low-cost haptic calibration method that improved 2D reaching accuracy of the humanoid robot NICO. In this paper, we extend this approach from reaching to tabletop object grasping by adding YOLO-based object and hand detection, stereo vision-based localization using the robot's built-in low-resolution fisheye cameras, and task-specific corrections for grasp execution. Together, these components form a novel calibration-based grasping pipeline that does not require RGB-D cameras, motion capture, or external tracking systems. We also implemented a visual feedback model that aligns the robot hand with the detected object before grasping. Our results show that the fully nonlinear calibration model achieved the best performance inside the calibrated area, while the visual feedback model achieved the highest overall grasping success across the full tabletop workspace.
candidates.author: Juraj Gavura, Igor Farkaš

candidates.title: Learning Adaptive Safety Margins for Visual Navigation
candidates.description: Robots in cluttered indoor spaces often fail not because they cannot generate collision-free paths, but because a fixed safety margin is mis-calibrated: conservative margins cause detours and timeouts, while permissive margins lead to near-boundary shortcuts under perception bias. Diffusion-based planners propose diverse trajectory candidates from egocentric RGB-D, yet reliable selection remains the bottleneck. We propose a context-conditioned safety critic that learns an adaptive clearance preference for ranking diffusion proposals, decomposed into three complementary terms: (i) a safety term with a clearance-budget penalty and a control-barrier-function residual for waypoint- and transition-wise safety, (ii) an efficiency term combining a smoothness penalty with a safety-gated detour-ratio penalty that avoids detours without incentivizing risky shortcuts, and (iii) a distance-constraint matching term that anchors the learned budget to realized ESDF clearances to prevent margin collapse. We train the critic with privileged ESDF geometry in simulation and distill it into a perception-only selector via a two-stage teacher-student procedure. On PointGoal navigation in HM3D and MP3D, including cross-dataset transfer, our method achieves the highest success rate (SR) and success weighted by path length (SPL) among strong diffusion, optimization, and RL baselines. Trained purely in simulation, it transfers to a Unitree G1 humanoid and navigates cluttered indoor scenes without task-specific tuning.
candidates.author: Junyi Hu, Shuaihang Yuan, Geeta Chandra Raju Bethala, Anthony Tzes, Yi Fang

candidates.title: PPL-Factory: Task-Aware and Budget-Aware Data Selection from Language Modeling to Reasoning
candidates.description: Not all training samples contribute equally to large language model fine-tuning. Selecting informative training samples can reduce the computational cost while preserving downstream performance. Many existing data selection methods rely on indirect heuristics, such as data quality, diversity or reasoning trace length. However, the effectiveness of these fixed criteria is task-dependent and difficult to generalize across diverse downstream tasks. Perplexity-based data selection provides a simple and model-aware solution to estimate the sample difficulty, but existing approaches typically score the entire training sequence and ignore the difference in learning objectives of language modeling and reasoning tasks. In this paper, we propose PPL-Factory, a simple and interpretable data selection framework that combines task-aware perplexity-based scores and data budget-aware selection criteria. Experiments on GSM8K demonstrate that PPL-Factory outperforms other state-of-the-art data selection methods using only $1\%$ of the training set. With $10\%$ of the data, PPL-Factory exceeds full-data fine-tuning accuracy by 0.9 on GSM8K and 4.8 on MATH. Overall, our results demonstrate that task-aware and budget-aware perplexity-based selection provides an effective and applicable approach for efficient fine-tuning.
candidates.author: Hang Zhang, Warren J. Gross

candidates.title: Imitation of Arm Gestures by the Semi-Humanoid Robot NICO
candidates.description: Seamless human-robot interaction (HRI) requires a number of perceptual and motor abilities from the robot, one of them being the imitation of human gestures. Humanoid robots have an advantage in HRI thanks to their anthropomorphic features. In this work, we develop a system for imitation of human arm gestures by the semi-humanoid robot NICO based on analytical geometry and a pretrained MediaPipe pose-estimation model. For each input RGB frame, 3D coordinates of relevant human body landmarks, including arm joints and hand keypoints, are obtained using the MediaPipe framework. Joint angles are then computed from these coordinates using derived geometric relations. Finally, the computed angles are properly mapped to NICO's motor configuration and executed in a predefined motion sequence. Preliminary experiments on several representative arm gestures with six participants of different height indicate that the proposed method can produce meaningful imitative motions from monocular RGB input only, while also highlighting limitations in more complex poses and wrist-related movements.
candidates.author: Anastasiya Ihnatovich, Igor Farkaš
